q: State Epictetus's core principle about what is and is not 'up to us', with a verbatim quote from the corpus.
final:
Epictetus’s core principle is the “dichotomy of control”: distinguish between what is in our power and what is not, and focus only on the former.

A verbatim quote from the corpus is:

> “if it concerns anything not in our power, be ready to say, ‘It does not concern me.’”

The corpus also summarizes the idea as:

> “Distinguish what is subject to us and what is not subject to us — and live only in the first.”

trace.final_text:
Epictetus’s core principle is the “dichotomy of control”: distinguish between what is in our power and what is not, and focus only on the former.

A verbatim quote from the corpus is:

> “if it concerns anything not in our power, be ready to say, ‘It does not concern me.’”

The corpus also summarizes the idea as:

> “Distinguish what is subject to us and what is not subject to us — and live only in the first.”
